title: Mock Exam B
description: A 22-task RHCSA9 mock exam covering persistent networking, repositories, users, services, storage, NFS, SSH, and rootless containers across client and server.

tasks: On client, recover root access from the console and set the root password to cinder9.

On client, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.41/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: client-b.exam9.lab
On client, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On client, install lsof from the configured repositories and remove tcpdump if it is installed.
On client, create group opsb9. Create users anab9, devb9, and auditb9; auditb9 must use /sbin/nologin. Set each password to cinder9 and add anab9 and devb9 to opsb9.
On client, set maximum password age 60 days and warning period 7 days for anab9. Allow members of opsb9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On client, create /srv/opsb9 owned by root:opsb9 with permissions 2770 and a default ACL granting opsb9 full access.

On client, collect passwd and profile configuration files into /root/review-b9, create bzip2 archive /root/review-b9.tar.bz2, and write the root passwd entry plus birch beacon report to /root/passwd-root-b9.txt.
On client, create a 512 MiB swap file at /swapb9, activate it immediately, and make it persistent.
On client, create volume group vgb9 on /dev/sdb, create logical volume datab9 with size 320 MiB, format it as XFS, and mount it persistently at /mnt/datab9.
On client, create user podb9, enable lingering for that user, and run a rootless container named webb9 from localhost/rhcsa-httpd-base:latest.

On server, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.3/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: server-b.exam9.lab
On server, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On server, create group srvb9, create user svcb9 with password cinder9, add svcb9 to srvb9, and allow srvb9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On server, publish /var/www/html/exam-b.html containing birch delta web, configure httpd to listen on TCP port 8301, label the port for httpd, and open it permanently in firewalld.
On server, enable persistent systemd journal storage and restart systemd-journald.
On server, schedule a root cron job that runs every 6 minutes and appends birch relay cron to /var/log/auditb9.log.
On server, set the default boot target to multi-user.target and create /srv/server-b9 owned by root:srvb9 with permissions 2770.
On server, export /exports/rhcsa9-b to 192.168.122.0/24 with a README containing birch storage export. On client, mount server:/exports/rhcsa9-b persistently at /mnt/rhcsa9-b.
On server, create user copyb9 with password cinder9. On client, configure key-based SSH login for root to copyb9@server.
On client, create /root/exam-b-rsync.txt containing birch archive transfer and use rsync over SSH to copy it to server:/home/copyb9/inbox/exam-b-rsync.txt.
On server, enable chronyd for the lab network. On client, configure chronyd to use server as its only time source.
solution_commands: # At the boot menu, edit the selected kernel entry.
# Append rw init=/bin/bash to the linux line and boot with Ctrl+x.
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.41/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.3/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
checks: echo cinder9 | su - root -c 'whoami' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx root

hostnamectl --static | grep -qx client-b.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.41/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3

test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '^enabled=1$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '^gpgcheck=0$' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]
rpm -q lsof >/dev/null

getent group opsb9 >/dev/null && id -nG anab9 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsb9 && id -nG devb9 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx opsb9 && getent passwd auditb9 | awk -F: '$7 == "/sbin/nologin" {found=1} END {exit !found}'

chage -l anab9 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*60' && chage -l anab9 | grep -Eq 'warning.*7' && grep -Eq '^%opsb9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$' /etc/sudoers.d/opsb9-systemctl
stat -c '%U:%G:%a' /srv/opsb9 | grep -qx root:opsb9:2770 && getfacl -p /srv/opsb9 | grep -Eq '^default:group:opsb9:rwx$'

test -s /root/review-b9.tar.bz2 && tar -tjf /root/review-b9.tar.bz2 | grep -qx 'review-b9/passwd' && tar -tjf /root/review-b9.tar.bz2 | grep -qx 'review-b9/profile' && grep -Fxq 'birch beacon report' /root/passwd-root-b9.txt && grep -Eq '^root:' /root/passwd-root-b9.txt
swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx '/swapb9' && awk '$1 == "/swapb9" && $2 == "swap" && $3 == "swap" {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
lvs /dev/vgb9/datab9 >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET /mnt/datab9 | grep -qx /mnt/datab9 && awk '$2 == "/mnt/datab9" && $3 == "xfs" {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab
runuser -l podb9 -c 'podman ps --format {{.Names}}' | grep -qx webb9 && loginctl show-user podb9 | grep -Eq '^Linger=yes$'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; hostnamectl --static | grep -qx server-b.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.3/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^enabled=1$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^gpgcheck=0$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group srvb9 >/dev/null && id -nG svcb9 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx srvb9 && grep -Eq '"'"'^%srvb9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$'"'"' /etc/sudoers.d/srvb9-systemctl'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq '"'"'birch delta web'"'"' /var/www/html/exam-b.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8301$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-b.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8301([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8301/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -x /usr/local/sbin/auditb9.sh && grep -Fxq '"'"'*/6 * * * * root /usr/local/sbin/auditb9.sh'"'"' /etc/cron.d/auditb9 && grep -Fxq "echo '"'"'birch relay cron'"'"' >> /var/log/auditb9.log" /usr/local/sbin/auditb9.sh && systemctl is-enabled crond | grep -qx enabled'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl get-default | grep -qx multi-user.target && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/server-b9 | grep -qx root:srvb9:2770'

findmnt -no SOURCE,TARGET /mnt/rhcsa9-b | grep -qx 'server:/exports/rhcsa9-b /mnt/rhcsa9-b' && grep -Eq '^server:/exports/rhcsa9-b[[:space:]]+/mnt/rhcsa9-b[[:space:]]+nfs([[:space:]]|$)' /etc/fstab && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq "birch storage export" /exports/rhcsa9-b/README && grep -Eq "^/exports/rhcsa9-b[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/rhcsa9-b.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'
runuser -l root -c 'ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o NumberOfPasswordPrompts=0 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null copyb9@server true'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq '"'"'birch archive transfer'"'"' /home/copyb9/inbox/exam-b-rsync.txt'

grep -Eq '^server[[:space:]]+server[[:space:]]+iburst$' /etc/chrony.conf && systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active chronyd | grep -qx active'
